for a better user experience i want to make sure in each step the context has a validate data from previous setp if NOT i shoud send user back to previous step and alert him for not having complete or valid data.

this is a snapshot of the code i have

btw it works when i put it inside a setTimeout

shadcn-ui/ui#7166.body:
### Describe the bug [https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/tooltip.tsx](url) The documentation for Tooltips is as follows: ``` import { Tooltip, TooltipContent, TooltipProvider, TooltipTrigger, } from "@/components/ui/tooltip" ``` ``` Hover

Add to library

 ``` However, the implementation of `Tooltip` has a redundant `TooltipProvider` implemented if you follow the documentation. ``` function Tooltip({ ...props }: React.ComponentProps) { return ( ) } ``` This caused a significant performance hit in my application which was fixed by removing the duplicate `TooltipProvider` implementation on every `Tooltip` instance. ### Affected component/components Tooltip ### How to reproduce Implement 60 `Tooltip` components in a canvas application. ### Codesandbox/StackBlitz link _No response_ ### Logs ```bash ``` ### System Info ```bash Any system (macOS). It is in the source code itself. ``` ### Before submitting - [x] I've made research efforts and searched the documentation - [x] I've searched for existing issues
shadcn-ui/ui#7166.title: [bug]: Tooltip has redundant TooltipProvider based on documentation.

shadcn-ui/ui#7348.body:
### Describe the bug



When I add a new shadcn component, I want the import { cn } that the component uses to be at a different location. I try to set my component.json to the following:

```json:components.json
//components.json
{
  "aliases": {
    "utils": "src/utils",
    "components": "src/components",
    "ui": "src/ui",
    "hooks": "src/hooks"
  }
}
```

But my new component always shows up (in the right ui folder though) with the wrong utils import:

import { cn } from "src/lib/utils"

### Affected component/components

component.json

### How to reproduce

1. I'm using a turbo repo mono repo with create-t3-stack at https://github.com/t3-oss/create-t3-turbo so you could clone this

2. Cd into the root folder

3. Cd into packages/ui

4. Try to modify the components.json similar to the above (with "utils": "src/utils")

5. Try to add any new shadcn component - you will see it defaults imports { cn } from "src/lib/utils"

### Codesandbox/StackBlitz link

_No response_

### Logs

```bash

```

### System Info

```bash
Cursor, Macbook Air M4 latest OS
```

### Before submitting

- [x] I've made research efforts and searched the documentation
- [x] I've searched for existing issues
shadcn-ui/ui#7348.title: [bug]: Adding new components default import { cn } from "src/lib/utils" even though component.json specify differently

shadcn-ui/ui#7583.body:
### Describe the bug

**Bug Description**
Date picker dropdown values are not visible when the application is in dark mode, making it impossible for users to select dates.
**Expected Behavior**
Date picker dropdown should display all selectable dates/values with proper contrast and visibility in dark mode, similar to how they appear in light mode.
**Actual Behavior**
Date picker dropdown values are not visible in dark mode - they appear to be rendered but with insufficient contrast or incorrect color styling.

**Steps to Reproduce**

Switch application to dark mode
Open/click on the date picker component
Observe that dropdown values are not visible

**Environment**

Browser: Chrome
OS: Linux Ubuntu

**Additional Context**
This appears to be a CSS styling issue where the dark mode theme is not properly applied to the date picker dropdown elements, or the text/background colors have an insufficient contrast ratio.

### Affected component/components

https://ui.shadcn.com/docs/components/date-picker

### How to reproduce

Switch application to dark mode
Open/click on the date picker component
Observe that dropdown values are not visible

### Codesandbox/StackBlitz link

_No response_

### Logs

```bash
Browser: Chrome
OS: Linux Ubuntu
```

### System Info

```bash
Browser: Chrome
OS: Linux Ubuntu
```

### Before submitting

- [x] I've made research efforts and searched the documentation
- [x] I've searched for existing issues
shadcn-ui/ui#7583.title: [bug]: Date Picker Dropdown values not visible in the Dark Mode

shadcn-ui/ui#7695.body:
### Describe the bug

Hi,

When I try to create a new monorepo project using shadcn with Bun, I encounter the following error:

Command: bunx --bun shadcn@canary init

Steps:
	•	The CLI prompts me to select a project type, and I choose “Next.js (Monorepo)”
	•	I enter a project name (e.g., Monoapp)
	•	The process fails with the error message below

```
Error output:✖ Something went wrong creating a new Next.js monorepo.
Something went wrong. Please check the error below for more details.
If the problem persists, please open an issue on GitHub.

Command failed with exit code 1: bun install
Resolving dependencies
Resolved, downloaded and extracted [2]
error: Workspace dependency "@workspace/eslint-config" not found

Searched in "./*"

Workspace documentation: https://bun.sh/docs/install/workspaces

error: Workspace dependency "@workspace/typescript-config" not found

Searched in "./*"

Workspace documentation: https://bun.sh/docs/install/workspaces

error: @workspace/eslint-config@workspace:* failed to resolve
error: @workspace/typescript-config@workspace:* failed to resolve
bun install v1.2.4 (fd9a5ea6)
```

Is there a workaround or something I might be missing in the setup? Thank you for your help!

### Affected component/components

Project

### How to reproduce

1. bunx --bun shadcn@canary init
2. Name your project

### Codesandbox/StackBlitz link

_No response_

```bash

```

```bash
• shadcn version: canary
```

shadcn-ui/ui#8367.body:
### Describe the bug

In the official docs the structure of the Empty component is shown as

```
<Empty>
  <EmptyHeader>
    <EmptyMedia variant="icon">
      <Icon />
    </EmptyMedia>
  </EmptyHeader>
  <EmptyTitle>No data</EmptyTitle>
  <EmptyDescription>No data found</EmptyDescription>
  <EmptyContent>
    <Button>Add data</Button>
  </EmptyContent>
</Empty>

```
when it should include the Title and Description in the Header to achieve the same Spacing as in the Examples.

```
<Empty>
  <EmptyHeader>
    <EmptyMedia variant="icon">
      <Icon />
    </EmptyMedia>
  <EmptyTitle>No data</EmptyTitle>
  <EmptyDescription>No data found</EmptyDescription>
  </EmptyHeader>
  <EmptyContent>
    <Button>Add data</Button>
  </EmptyContent>
</Empty>
```

### Affected component/components

Empty

### How to reproduce

Go on the official Website https://ui.shadcn.com/docs/components/empty

### Codesandbox/StackBlitz link

_No response_

### Logs

```bash

```

### System Info

```bash
Chrome
```

### Before submitting

- [x] I've made research efforts and searched the documentation
- [x] I've searched for existing issues
shadcn-ui/ui#8367.title: [bug]: In the docs the description of the Empty Component is wrongly structured

shadcn-ui/ui#8425.body:
### Describe the bug

## Issue
- the import statement for the `Link` is incorrect in the snippet that explains how to add Link in the Button.
- [Snippet Link](https://ui.shadcn.com/docs/components/button#link-1) 

So this creates an error when the snippet is copied and used

## Solution
- replace the import statement with `import Link from "next/link"`

### Affected component/components

Button

### How to reproduce

1. Go to [snippet link ](https://ui.shadcn.com/docs/components/button#link-1)
2. copy the snippet
3. Paste it in a nextjs project
4 You will be prompted with an error `Module '"next/link"' has no exported member 'Link'. Did you mean to use 'import Link from "next/link"' instead?`

### Codesandbox/StackBlitz link

_No response_

### Logs

```bash

```

### System Info

```bash
Chrome (version - 141.0.7390.76)
Nextjs (version - 15)
```

### Before submitting

- [x] I've made research efforts and searched the documentation
- [x] I've searched for existing issues
shadcn-ui/ui#8425.title: [bug]: import statement for Link is incorrect in a snippet of button component docs

shadcn-ui/ui#8432.body: ### Describe the bug I'm trying to open my registry for some useful files, but without framework detection & components.json `'use client'` directive is removed from tsx , assuming rsc is false. My registry.json ```json { "name": "color-picker", "type": "registry:item", "title": "Color Picker", "description": "A customizable color picker field for PayloadCMS.", "files": [ { "path": "registry/fields/color-picker/field.ts", "type": "registry:file", "target": "~/src/fields/color-picker/field.ts" }, { "path": "registry/fields/color-picker/index.tsx", "type": "registry:file", "target": "~/src/fields/color-picker/index.tsx" }, { "path": "registry/fields/color-picker/style.scss", "type": "registry:file", "target": "~/src/fields/color-picker/style.scss" }, { "path": "registry/fields/color-picker/types.ts", "type": "registry:file", "target": "~/src/fields/color-picker/types.ts" } ] } ``` Going into URL manually i can see `use client`, but adding files from this registry removes it. ### Affected component/components CLI ### How to reproduce 1.Try to add components from this registry - https://payload.veiag.dev/r/color-picker.json 2.`index.tsx` content saved without `'use client'` JSON: Saved file: ### Codesandbox/StackBlitz link _No response_ ### Logs ```bash ``` ### System Info ```bash Node v22.16.0 latest shadcn package ``` ### Before submitting - [x] I've made research efforts and searched the documentation - [x] I've searched for existing issues
shadcn-ui/ui#8432.title: [bug]: CLI Registry universal items still removes 'use client' from tsx files

shadcn-ui/ui#8450.body:
### Describe the bug

When a registry item has an at-property rule, the `npx shadcn@latest add` command throws the following error:

```
Error parsing rule content: syntax "<number>" CssSyntaxError: <css input>:1:7: Unknown word "<number>"
    at Input.error (/.npm/_npx/d66c5096c7023bfb/node_modules/postcss/lib/input.js:135:16)
    at Parser.unknownWord (/.npm/_npx/d66c5096c7023bfb/node_modules/postcss/lib/parser.js:595:22)
    at Parser.other (/.npm/_npx/d66c5096c7023bfb/node_modules/postcss/lib/parser.js:437:12)
    at Parser.parse (/.npm/_npx/d66c5096c7023bfb/node_modules/postcss/lib/parser.js:472:16)
    at Function.parse (/.npm/_npx/d66c5096c7023bfb/node_modules/postcss/lib/parse.js:11:12)
    at re (file:///.npm/_npx/d66c5096c7023bfb/node_modules/shadcn/dist/index.js:20:241)
    at Mt (file:///.npm/_npx/d66c5096c7023bfb/node_modules/shadcn/dist/index.js:15:220)
    at Object.Once (file:///.npm/_npx/d66c5096c7023bfb/node_modules/shadcn/dist/index.js:14:65)
    at LazyResult.runOnRoot (/.npm/_npx/d66c5096c7023bfb/node_modules/postcss/lib/lazy-result.js:359:23)
    at LazyResult.runAsync (/.npm/_npx/d66c5096c7023bfb/node_modules/postcss/lib/lazy-result.js:290:26) {
  reason: 'Unknown word "<number>"',
  source: '.temp{"<number>"}',
  line: 1,
  column: 7,
  endLine: 1,
  endColumn: 17,
  input: {
    column: 7,
    endColumn: 17,
    endLine: 1,
    endOffset: 16,
    line: 1,
    offset: 6,
    source: '.temp{"<number>"}'
  }
}


Something went wrong. Please check the error below for more details.
If the problem persists, please open an issue on GitHub.

update-css: <css input>:1:7: Unknown word "<number>"
```

### Affected component/components

add command

### How to reproduce

1. Create an `example-at-property.json` file:

    ```json
    {
      "$schema": "https://ui.shadcn.com/schema/registry-item.json",
      "name": "example-at-property",
      "type": "registry:style",
      "css": {
        "@property --foo": {
          "syntax": "'<number>'",
          "inherits": "false",
          "initial-value": "0"
        }
      }
    }
    ```

2. Run `npx shadcn@latest add example-at-property.json --yes` in a project

3. See error

### Codesandbox/StackBlitz link

_No response_

### Logs

```bash

```

### System Info

```bash
macOS 26, Node 22
```

### Before submitting

- [x] I've made research efforts and searched the documentation
- [x] I've searched for existing issues

### Name

@shadcnui-blocks

### URL

https://www.shadcnui-blocks.com/r/{name}

### Homepage

https://www.shadcnui-blocks.com/

### Description

A collection of premium, production-ready shadcn/ui blocks, components and templates.

### Logo

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/0bef0dae-ee5c-4c7b-b593-88503ceee537)

### Checklist

- [x] The registry must be open source and publicly accessible.
- [x] The registry must be a valid JSON file that conforms to the [registry schema](https://ui.shadcn.com/docs/registry/registry-json) specification.
- [x] The `files` array, if present on your registry items, must NOT include a `content` property.
- [x] I've attached a square SVG logo to this issue

shadcn-ui/ui#8705.body:
On macOS, two-finger swipe navigation (back/forward) doesn’t work when using the following global CSS rule
`overscroll-behavior: none;` defined in [apps/v4/styles/globals.css](https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/86c494c452c0e4a923832de9135e45c83f55b33f/apps/v4/styles/globals.css#L153)￼.

Here's a suggestion
```
overscroll-y-none
```
shadcn-ui/ui#8705.title: [bug]: two-finger navigation broken on macOS

### Homepage

### Description

Modular shadcn registry for CodeRabbit API integration. Includes a framework-agnostic API client, pluggable storage adapters (LocalStorage, Convex, Supabase, PostgreSQL, MySQL), and React components for generating developer activity reports. Supports multiple storage backends with built-in TypeScript types and zero runtime dependencies for the core client.

shadcn-ui/ui#9118.body:
### Describe the bug

Hi Everyone : D

After updating dependencies, the shadcn/ui Resizable component seems to behave incorrectly.

It looks related to an update in the underlying library (react-resizable-panels).
Before the update, the same layout worked as expected.

I created a minimal reproduction to demonstrate the issue.

Thanks for the great project!


### Affected component/components

Resizable

### How to reproduce

Steps to reproduce:

1. Add the Resizable component from the shadcn/ui documentation.
2. Use the Resizable component with a basic example
3. App crashes

Expected behavior:
Resizable panels behave correctly as before.

Actual behavior:
The app stops working due to not found components


### Codesandbox/StackBlitz link

https://codesandbox.io/p/devbox/3s558d

### Logs

```bash
## Error Type
Build Error

## Error Message
Export PanelGroup doesn't exist in target module

## Build Output
./components/ui/resizable.tsx:4:1
Export PanelGroup doesn't exist in target module
  2 |
  3 | import { GripVertical } from "lucide-react"
> 4 | import * as ResizablePrimitive from "react-resizable-panels"
    | ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
  5 |
  6 | import { cn } from "@/lib/utils"
  7 |

The export PanelGroup was not found in module [project]/node_modules/.pnpm/react-resizable-panels@4.0.1_react-dom@19.0.0_react@19.0.0/node_modules/react-resizable-panels/dist/react-resizable-panels.js [app-client] (ecmascript).
Did you mean to import Panel?
All exports of the module are statically known (It doesn't have dynamic exports). So it's known statically that the requested export doesn't exist.

Import trace:
  Server Component:
    ./components/ui/resizable.tsx
    ./components/resizableDemo.tsx
    ./app/page.tsx

Next.js version: 15.5.7 (Turbopack)
```

### System Info

```bash
OS : Windows 11
Browser : Microsoft Edge
Front : Next.Js
```

### Before submitting

- [x] I've made research efforts and searched the documentation
- [x] I've searched for existing issues

shadcn-ui/ui#9160.body:
### Describe the bug
When creating a new project using `npx shadcn@latest create`, the process fails if the project name contains uppercase letters (e.g. `Website`).

The CLI allows entering uppercase characters, but does not validate or normalize the project name before attempting to create the Next.js app. This results in a generic error message:

> Something went wrong creating a new Next.js project. Please try again.

Using the same command with a lowercase project name (e.g. `website`) works correctly.

This behavior is confusing because:
- the CLI accepts uppercase letters without warning
- the error message does not explain the actual cause

---


### Affected component/components

None

### How to reproduce

1. Open a terminal
2. Run: ``npx shadcn@latest create --preset "https://ui.shadcn.com/init?base=radix&style=vega&baseColor=stone&theme=lime&iconLibrary=lucide&font=figtree&menuAccent=subtle&menuColor=inverted&radius=none&template=next" --template next``
3. When prompted for the project name, enter a name containing uppercase letters (e.g. `Project`)
4. Press Enter
5. The CLI fails with the message:
   "Something went wrong creating a new Next.js project. Please try again."
6. Run the same command again
7. Enter a lowercase project name (e.g. `project`)
8. The project is created successfully


### Codesandbox/StackBlitz link

_No response_

### Logs

```bash
√ What is your project named? ... Website
| Creating a new Next.js project. This may take a few minutes.
Something went wrong creating a new Next.js project. Please try again

√ What is your project named? ... website
√ Creating a new Next.js project.
√ Writing components.json.
√ Checking registry.
√ Updating CSS variables in app\globals.css
√ Updating app\globals.css
√ Installing dependencies.
√ Updating fonts.
√ Created 1 file:
  - lib\utils.ts

Success! Project initialization completed.
```

### System Info

```bash
Node Version: v25.1.0
NPM Version: 11.6.2
OS: Windows 11
```

### Before submitting

- [x] I've made research efforts and searched the documentation
- [x] I've searched for existing issues
shadcn-ui/ui#9160.title: [bug]: Project creation fails when project name contains uppercase letters

shadcn-ui/ui#9222.body:
### Describe the bug

The Bug Report Form example in the TanStack Form docs (https://ui.shadcn.com/docs/forms/tanstack-form) has noticeable input lag when typing in the description textarea field.

## Root Cause

The form is configured with `validators: { onSubmit: formSchema }` at the form level, which causes Zod to validate the entire form schema on every keystroke. Combined with reading `field.state.meta` properties (`isTouched`, `isValid`, `errors`) in the render function, this creates expensive validation + re-render cycles for each character typed.

## Performance Impact

- Users experience visible delay when typing in the description textarea
- The lag becomes more noticeable as the field value grows
- This creates a poor user experience and doesn't reflect best practices for form performance

## Suggested Fix

Change validation strategy to one of:

1. Use `onBlur` validation instead of real-time for the description field:
```tsx
validators: {
  onSubmit: formSchema,
  onBlur: formSchema,
}
```

2. Or use field-level validators for better granularity:
```tsx
<form.Field
  name="description"
  validators={{
    onBlur: ({ value }) => {
      const result = formSchema.shape.description.safeParse(value)
      return result.error?.errors?.[0]?.message
    },
  }}TanStack Form docs example
>
```

This would make the example both more performant and demonstrate better practices for handling expensive validations.

### Affected component/components

TanStack Form docs example

### How to reproduce

1. Visit https://ui.shadcn.com/docs/forms/tanstack-form
2. Scroll to the "Bug Report Form" example
3. Click on the "Description" textarea field
4. Start typing continuously (e.g., "This is a test of the textarea performance...")
5. Observe noticeable input lag/delay between keystrokes, especially as the text length grows

**Expected behavior:** Typing should feel instant and responsive with no lag

**Actual behavior:** Each keystroke has a visible delay as Zod validates the entire form schema on every character input

### Codesandbox/StackBlitz link

_No response_

### Logs

```bash

```

### System Info

```bash
System: macOS
Browser: Chrome
Node: N/A (browser-based issue)

This performance issue is reproducible in any modern browser when using the TanStack Form example from the docs.
```

### Before submitting

- [x] I've made research efforts and searched the documentation
- [x] I've searched for existing issues
shadcn-ui/ui#9222.title: [bug]: TanStack Form example has poor performance - textarea input lag due to real-time validation

shadcn-ui/ui#9370.body:
### Describe the bug

The `@motion-primitives` registry entry in the shadcn registry directory uses `https://motion-primitives.com/c/registry.json` which is missing the required `{name}` placeholder. This causes the CLI to fail validation when resolving components that have `@motion-primitives/*` as a `registryDependency`.

**Additional context:**

The registry was added via Issue #8588 with URL `https://motion-primitives.com/c/registry.json`.

### Affected component/components

Registry Directory / CLI namespace resolution

### How to reproduce

1. Create a component with `@motion-primitives/text-effect` as a `registryDependency`
2. Run `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add your-component`
3. CLI fails with: `Validation failed: - registries: Registry URL must include {name} placeholder`

or 

1. Run `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @motion-primitives/text-effect`

### Codesandbox/StackBlitz link

N/A - This is a registry directory configuration issue, not a code issue. The bug is in the stored URL for `@motion-primitives` in the registry directory.

### Logs

```bash
pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @tailark-pro/dark-landing-one

Something went wrong. Please check the error below for more details.
If the problem persists, please open an issue on GitHub.

Validation failed:
- registries: Registry URL must include {name} placeholder
```

### System Info

```bash
- shadcn CLI version: 3.7.0
- Node: v20.x
- OS: macOS
```

### Before submitting

- [x] I've made research efforts and searched the documentation
- [x] I've searched for existing issues
shadcn-ui/ui#9370.title: [bug]: @motion-primitives registry URL missing {name} placeholder causes CLI validation failure

shadcn-ui/ui#9635.body:
### Describe the bug

Could you please reorder the className and variant props in the Badge component? Right now, user-provided classes are always being overridden, which causes them to lose precedence. Reordering these should allow user classes to take priority and resolve the issue.
it is in base-ui
https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/2ef58bd75d6d9300e155908062c8504c40017a78/apps/v4/registry/bases/base/ui/badge.tsx#L36
### Affected component/components

Badge

### How to reproduce

**How to reproduce**

1. Create a Badge using the existing component.
2. Pass a custom class such as `rounded-sm` through the `className` prop.
3. Notice that the style does not apply — the default styles from the component override it.
4. The custom class only works if you force it with `rounded-sm!`.

After checking the component implementation, I found this line:

```ts
className: cn(badgeVariants({ className, variant })),
```

Because `className` is passed before `variant`, the generated variant styles take precedence over user-provided classes.

Normally, it should be ordered like this:

```ts
className: cn(badgeVariants({ variant, className })),
```

This way, user classes are applied last and can properly override the defaults.


### Codesandbox/StackBlitz link

_No response_

### Logs

```bash

```

### System Info

```bash
react base-ui
```

### Before submitting

- [x] I've made research efforts and searched the documentation

shadcn-ui/ui#9651.body: ### Describe the bug Using the `` with "base-ui" and the `disabled` props on `` doesn't apply style for the trigger/button. It's because actually the classes on `` are `disabled:pointer-events-none disabled:opacity-50` but base-ui only apply an `aria-disabled` attribute (not directly the `disabled` attr on the button). The solution is to also add `aria-disabled:pointer-events-none aria-disabled:opacity-50` on the style on `` ### Affected component/components Accordion ### How to reproduce Example from create with radix: https://ui.shadcn.com/create?item=accordion-example&iconLibrary=hugeicons&font=geist Example from create with base: https://ui.shadcn.com/create?item=accordion-example&iconLibrary=hugeicons&font=geist&base=base ### Codesandbox/StackBlitz link _No response_ ### Logs ```bash ``` ### System Info ```bash System: OS: macOS 15.5 CPU: (14) arm64 Apple M4 Pro Memory: 178.59 MB / 24.00 GB Shell: 5.9 - /bin/zsh Binaries: Node: 22.14.0 - /Users/xxxxx/.volta/tools/image/node/22.14.0/bin/node npm: 10.9.2 - /Users/xxxxx/.volta/tools/image/node/22.14.0/bin/npm Browsers: Chrome: 145.0.7632.76 Firefox: 147.0 Firefox Developer Edition: 148.0 ``` ### Before submitting - [x] I've made research efforts and searched the documentation - [x] I've searched for existing issues
shadcn-ui/ui#9651.title: [bug]: missing disabled style for Accordion with base-ui

shadcn-ui/ui#9657.body:
### Describe the bug

Hello, @shadcn 

I am using the manual installation method of shadcn/ui because I’m working with React + Vite + WXT, which is not natively supported by the CLI.

In the documentation (Step 4 of Manual Installation):
[](https://ui.shadcn.com/docs/installation/manual)

The src/styles/globals.css file includes the following imports:
```
@import "tailwindcss";
@import "tw-animate-css";
@import "shadcn/tailwind.css";
```

However, with Tailwind CSS v4, adding all three imports throws an error.
In v4, only the following is required:
`@import "tailwindcss";`

The other imports are no longer needed and cause conflicts.

Suggested Fix:
Please update the documentation to reflect Tailwind v4 compatibility and clarify that only:
`@import "tailwindcss";`

Thank you!

### Affected component/components

Manual installation Document

### How to reproduce

1) Go to https://ui.shadcn.com/docs/installation/manual
2) In this link see **step-4** Configure styles

### Codesandbox/StackBlitz link

_No response_

### Logs

```bash

```

### System Info

```bash
All Browsers
```

### Before submitting

- [x] I've made research efforts and searched the documentation
- [x] I've searched for existing issues
shadcn-ui/ui#9657.title: [bug]:Need to Update the Manual installation Document.

when attempting to change preset using the skill in Claude Code, it attempts to change from base-ui to radix because the preset maps to radix (i was using base-ui)

```
Claude: This is a significant change since it switches from Base UI to Radix primitives. Let me now update the config, CSS, and smart-merge each component.

Me: --preset aJMi5Dc is supposed to be base-vega not radix
```
my response is i guess false, since it shouldn't map to either?

```
Claude: The CLI resolved preset aJMi5Dc to radix-vega, not base-vega. The preset code determines the exact style — it seems this code maps to radix-vega in the shadcn registry.
```

shadcn-ui/ui#10072.body:
### Describe the bug

The shadcn skill installed via `npx skills add shadcn/ui` fails to load in Claude Code with this error:

```
Bash command permission check failed for pattern "!`npx shadcn@latest info --json 2>/dev/null || echo '{"error": "No shadcn project found. Run shadcn init first."}'`": This Bash command contains multiple operations. The following part requires approval: npx shadcn@latest info --json
```

The inline bash command on line 16 of `SKILL.md` uses `2>/dev/null || echo '...'` which Claude Code treats as "multiple operations" and blocks without showing an approval prompt to the user.

The command `npx shadcn@latest info --json` works perfectly when run directly in the terminal.

### Expected behavior

The skill should load successfully. Either:
- Claude Code should show an approval prompt for the inline command (like it does for regular bash commands)
- Or the SKILL.md should include `allowed-tools: Bash(npx shadcn@latest *)` in the frontmatter to pre-authorize the command

### Environment

- Claude Code: 2.1.76
- OS: macOS (Darwin 25.2.0)
- Node: v24.11.1
- Permission mode: default (not acceptEdits or bypassPermissions)
- Skill installed at project level via `npx skills add shadcn/ui`

### How to reproduce

1. Install the skill: `npx skills add shadcn/ui`
2. Open Claude Code in default permission mode
3. Invoke the skill (either directly or by working with shadcn components)
4. Error appears, skill fails to load

### Additional context

The `SKILL.md` frontmatter does not include `allowed-tools`. According to Claude Code docs, skills that use inline `!` bash commands should declare `allowed-tools` in frontmatter to authorize those commands.
shadcn-ui/ui#10072.title: [bug]: shadcn skill fails to load in Claude Code — inline bash command blocked by permission check

vercel/turborepo#11047.body:
### Verify canary release

- [x] I verified that the issue exists in the latest Turborepo canary release.

### Link to code that reproduces this issue

bunx --bun shadcn@latest init

### Which canary version will you have in your reproduction?

2.6.0 


### Expected behavior

This issue might not be directly related to Turbo, but it’s connected to how Bun handles isolated installs.

The project builds successfully with pnpm workspaces, but not with Bun, specifically when using isolated installs.

Here’s what’s happening:

The @tailwind/postcss plugin is installed only inside packages/ui, and that package exports the PostCSS config directly.

Since we are explicitly exporting the PostCSS config from ui, we shouldn’t need to install @tailwind/postcss (or related deps) again in web.

This approach keeps all Tailwind/PostCSS-related dependencies inside packages/ui — where the components actually live — instead of duplicating them in multiple places.

However, with Bun’s isolated install mode, it doesn’t work that way.
Bun requires the same packages (postcss, tailwindcss, and @tailwind/postcss) to also be installed inside web, or else the build fails.

With pnpm, everything works as expected 


Having Tailwind/PostCSS only inside ui makes it:

Easier to manage versions (single source of truth)

Cleaner for dependency management

Easier to maintain the shared UI package without extra installs

In the official example repo using TailwindCSS, you install the PostCSS/Tailwind packages in both:

packages/tailwindconfig

and again in apps/web

You even end up installing postcss inside both packages.

That setup technically works, but it adds unnecessary duplication.
If the PostCSS config is exported from one place, it should be reusable without needing to install those same packages again everywhere else.

This approach (keeping it in ui) also ensures all external package versions stay consistent, since there’s just one source of truth.



### Actual behavior


> Build error occurred
Error: Turbopack build failed with 1 errors:
./packages/ui/src/styles/globals.css
Error evaluating Node.js code
Error: Cannot find module '@tailwindcss/postcss'
Require stack:



Import trace:
  Client Component Browser:
    ./packages/ui/src/styles/globals.css [Client Component Browser]
    ./apps/web/app/layout.tsx [Server Component]


    at ignore-listed frames
 ELIFECYCLE  Command failed with exit code 1.


### To Reproduce
```
bun install
bun run build
```

### Additional context

_No response_
vercel/turborepo#11047.title: bun isolated installs are not stable with shadcn ui template
